sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés have had a mixed run in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, including a strong 7-0 cup victory. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, have shown resilience with 4 wins in their last 5 games across all competitions, though they suffered a 4-2 defeat against Barcelona recently. Alavés will look to capitalize on home advantage, while Celta's attacking momentum could pose a threat.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, the matches have been closely contested, with a 1-1 draw at Alavés in January 2025 and a 2-1 victory for Celta in August 2024. Both games saw goals from both sides, and historical data suggests over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 meetings, indicating a likelihood of an open game.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Alavés sit 10th in La Liga with 15 points, showing defensive solidity with only 11 goals conceded (3rd best in the league). Celta Vigo, 13th with 13 points, have struggled defensively (18 goals conceded) but have scored 15 goals, showing attacking potential. Alavés’ home form and defensive structure might give them an edge, but Celta’s knack for scoring could test their backline.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Alavés, Carlos Vicente and Mariano Díaz are in form, with recent goals and high Sofascore ratings. Celta’s Borja Iglesias, with 5 goals this season (5th in La Liga), will be a focal point in attack. Their individual contributions could sway the game’s outcome.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Alavés average 5.5 corners per game and focus on late goals (36% in the 76-90 minute range), suggesting a patient approach. Celta often score early (first to score in 6 of their last 7 games) and have seen both teams scoring in 8 of their last 10 matches, indicating an aggressive start. Expect Alavés to defend deep initially while Celta push for an early lead.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s probabilities (30% home win, 34% draw, 36% away win) and Celta’s recent attacking form, this match leans slightly towards the visitors. However, Alavés’ home defensive record and historical competitiveness suggest a tight contest, likely ending in a score draw. I predict a 1-1 result with high card count due to both teams’ physical style.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline remains goalless between Deportivo Alavés and Celta Vigo at Estadio de Mendizorroza. Neither team has managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments. Alavés have shown more dominance in possession and territorial play, while Celta Vigo have struggled to create significant threats. Both sides have had equal opportunities from corners, but the lack of clinical finishing has defined the first 45 minutes. With both teams picking up a yellow card each, discipline will be crucial moving forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance: Deportivo Alavés
halftimeSections.text: Deportivo Alavés have controlled much of the game, enjoying a slight edge in possession and showing greater intent in the final third. Their ability to win duels, particularly in the air, has been impressive, allowing them to maintain pressure. The team has made frequent entries into dangerous areas and won possession in advanced positions multiple times. However, their inability to convert these opportunities into clear chances is a concern. Players like Toni Martínez have shown potential to make an impact, and they will need to capitalize on their territorial dominance in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance: Celta Vigo
halftimeSections.text: Celta Vigo have been on the back foot for large parts of the first half, with less possession and fewer entries into threatening positions. Their struggles in duels, especially in the air, have limited their ability to sustain attacks. While they have shown moments of resilience defensively, their attacking output has been minimal, with very little to show in terms of creativity in the final third. Players like Iago Aspas will need to step up if they are to turn the tide, but their overall performance suggests they may need a tactical adjustment to find a way back into this game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Alavés started with a strong attacking spell early on, maintaining pressure for extended periods and consistently pushing forward. There were brief moments where Celta Vigo countered with intent, particularly in clusters during the middle and towards the end of the half, showing some promise in breaking through. However, Alavés quickly regained control with sustained attacking waves, especially in the latter stages before the break. This back-and-forth dynamic suggests that while Alavés have been more assertive, Celta Vigo are capable of dangerous bursts if given the chance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch in the second half
halftimeSections.text: The second half will likely hinge on Alavés’ ability to convert their dominance into goals, as they have shown far more promise in advanced areas. Their focus should be on sharpening their finishing and making the most of set-piece opportunities. For Celta Vigo, improving their presence in duels and finding ways to penetrate the final third will be critical if they hope to snatch a result. Both teams need to avoid unnecessary bookings, as discipline could play a role in a tightly contested match. Tactical substitutions could also shift the balance, with fresh legs potentially making the difference in breaking the deadlock.
